title: Plain-language "what this model is / is not" summary (education-only)
context: Technical model cards are written for researchers and ML practitioners. Patient advocates, patients, and non-specialist educators also need to understand what an open cancer ML model does and does not do. This task produces a plain-language education-only summary derived from an already-accepted technical card. It is classified riskTier:high because patient-facing content that misrepresents a model's purpose or limitations could cause harm. Zero patient-facing output ships ungated. Depends on card-013 (an accepted technical card) and expert-007 (secured oncology expert + patient-advocate reviewers).
objective: Produce a plain-language education-only summary explaining what one accepted open cancer ML model is and is not for, derived strictly from the accepted technical card, with credentialed oncologist sign-off and patient-advocate sign-off recorded before publication.
acceptanceCriteria: Summary is derived only from the already-accepted technical card (card-013 or a subsequent accepted card); it introduces no claims not present in the parent card's citations[].
Summary states in plain language what the model does, what it is not for, and its key limitations and biases; no diagnostic, prognostic, or treatment guidance is present or implied.
A prominent 'not medical advice' disclaimer and research-use-only framing appear at the top of the document; readers are explicitly directed to qualified clinicians for medical decisions.
Every factual claim in the summary traces to a specific entry in the parent technical card's citations[] index.

A credentialed oncologist (MD or equivalent with oncology expertise, as secured in expert-007) has reviewed and signed off the summary before publication; their name and sign-off date are recorded in a committed review artifact.
A patient advocate (as secured in expert-007) has reviewed and signed off the summary before publication; their name and sign-off date are recorded in the same committed review artifact.
The summary is published only after both sign-offs are recorded; the review artifact is committed alongside the summary document.
resources: PLAN.md — Patient-facing plain-language summaries; Quality, review & risk gates; Two-tier risk model
output: A plain-language education-only summary of one accepted open cancer ML model, committed with a review artifact recording oncologist and patient-advocate sign-offs, published only after both sign-offs are recorded.
